headline: 5 Solid Dividend Stocks to Buy in July
summary: Yield above 4% sounds appealing until you realize most high-payout stocks are traps waiting to spring. These five names span life insurance, asset management, banking, and consumer staples, and each one earns its dividend rather than just promising it.

raw.headline: 5 Solid Dividend Stocks to Buy in July

headline: Evercore ISI Group Maintains Underperform on Franklin Resources, Raises Price Target to $35
summary: Evercore ISI Group  analyst David Motemaden   maintains Franklin Resources (NYSE:BEN) with a Underperform and raises the price target from $32 to $35.

raw.headline: Evercore ISI Group Maintains Underperform on Franklin Resources, Raises Price Target to $35
raw.summary: Evercore ISI Group  analyst David Motemaden   maintains Franklin Resources (NYSE:BEN) with a Underperform and raises the price target from $32 to $35.

headline: Franklin Resources (BEN) Stock May Trade At A Premium After A 43% Run
summary: Franklin Resources has delivered a 42.9% total return over the past five years, and today the valuation picture is split, with the Excess Returns intrinsic value estimate pointing to upside while earnings-based multiples lean expensive. A 42.9% return over five years suggests Franklin Resources has already rewarded patient shareholders, so fresh buyers now need to think carefully about what is priced in. Recent momentum in earnings expectations may support the intrinsic value case, while any...

headline: Will Risk-Off Selling and Fed Jitters Change Franklin Resources' (BEN) Earnings-Momentum Narrative?
summary: In early July 2026, Franklin Resources was hit by a broad risk-off shift as geopolitical tensions and concerns about further Federal Reserve rate hikes pressured sentiment toward asset managers whose fee income and portfolio values are closely tied to market conditions. At the same time, the firm’s strong recent record of earning surprises and fresh senior leadership at Fiduciary Trust International highlight an underlying operational momentum that contrasts with the macro-driven caution...
